problem.heading: What's wrong with modern Minecraft?
problem.intro: Modern servers are either donation machines or kindergarten simulators. Everything revolves around ranks, perks and paid shortcuts. Progress is sold. Power is packaged. Gameplay becomes a product instead of an adventure.
redHeading: A thousand rules protecting everyone from everything.
problem.outro: No risk. No chaos. No stories. Just sanitized gameplay.

solution.heading: We chose depth over safety.
solution.intro: Minecraft 1.7.10 was the era of real progression. Tech that could backfire. Magic that required learning. Systems that rewarded intelligence.
solution.outro: And that’s exactly what this server is built on.

main.heading: Three layers of chaos. One world.
main.body: Magic, machinery and survival all collide in a world that actually fights back.

slides.title: Magic that feels dangerous
paragraphs: Thaumcraft 4 with addons, Botania and EvilCraft turn the world into something far stranger than vanilla ever was.
Research forbidden knowledge, build arcane systems, harness unstable power and push deeper into mechanics that reward curiosity instead of handholding.
This is not decorative fantasy. It is progression, experimentation and consequences.

slides.title: Technology with actual consequences
paragraphs: Thermal Expansion, IndustrialCraft, NuclearCraft and ICBM give you machines that do more than just look impressive.
Build production lines, manage power, automate everything, experiment with reactors and weaponize the world if you are reckless enough to try.

slides.title: A world that pushes back
paragraphs: With EnviroMine, realistic torches and Pam's HarvestCraft, survival is no longer background decoration.
Temperature matters. Food matters. Light matters. Preparation matters.
You are not just building in the world — you are dealing with it. And that makes every shelter, farm and expedition feel earned.
This is old-school modded tech — powerful, rewarding and absolutely capable of blowing up in your face.

join.heading: Think you can survive here?
join.body: We keep the whitelist small so the world doesn't turn into chaos. Drop your nickname and a few words about yourself.
